title: Opening and Closing Tags
instruction:
Almost every HTML element requires both an opening tag and a closing tag. If we forget the closing tag, the formatting 'leaks' and breaks the page.

Examples of opening tags are <u>, <i>, and <b>.

Examples of closing tags are </u>, </i>, and </b>.

Example:
<u>Underlined</u>

Your Task: Find the sentence that is incorrectly styled and fix the missing closing tag in the editor.
initialCode: Only this sentence should be underlined. Only this sentence should be italicized. Only this sentence should be in bold text.
successMsg: Wow, ikaw ay isang hackerist! Adding the </u> tag stops the underline from leaking into the next lines.

title: The Main Heading
instruction:
The <h1> tag is the most important heading on a page.

Example:
<h1>The Main Header</h1>

Your Task: Wrap 'Welcome to my Website' in an <h1> tag.
successMsg: Nays one! The h1 tag makes the text big and bold.

title: Subheadings
instruction:
Subheadings like <h2> are smaller than h1. They go all the way down to h6.

Example:
<h2>My Pets</h2>

Your Task: On line 2, wrap 'About Me' in an <h2> tag.
successMsg: Ayos! Notice how h2 is slightly smaller than h1.

title: Paragraphs
instruction:
The <p> tag is used for normal paragraph text.

Example:
<p>This is a sentence.</p>

Your Task: On line 4, wrap 'I am a student learning HTML today.' in a <p> tag.
initialCode:
Welcome to my Website

About Me

I am a student learning HTML today.
successMsg: Perfect! Now you have regular text.

title: Bold Text
instruction:
The <strong> tag makes text bold and important.

Example:

Read this: <strong>Important!</strong> — pay attention.


Your Task: Inside your paragraph, wrap the word 'HTML' in a <strong> tag. Note that ONLY the word requires the tag, not the whole sentence!
initialCode:
Welcome to my Website

About Me

I am a student learning HTML today.
successMsg: Awesome! The word stands out now.

title: Line Breaks
instruction:
Use the <br> tag when you want to start a new line without creating a new paragraph.

Example:
First line<br>Second line

Your Task: Put a line break between 'Hello' and 'World'.
successMsg: Nice! The line break moved the text to a new line.

title: Putting It All Together
instruction: Great job so far! Now, build a simple page layout using what you've learned. It needs a main title <h1> with your name, a sub header <h2> with your section, and a single sentence in a paragraph tag <p> of your favorite motto, quote, or bible verse.
validate:
return code.toLowerCase().includes('

') && code.toLowerCase().includes('

') && code.toLowerCase().includes('

') && code.toLowerCase().includes('

') && code.toLowerCase().includes('

') && code.toLowerCase().includes('

');
successMsg: Excellent! You have mastered the basics of page structure.

title: Ordered Lists
successMsg: Amazing! Numbered lists help organize steps.

title: Hyperlinks
instruction:
The <a> tag makes a clickable link. You must include the href (hyperlink reference) attribute to tell it where to go.

Example:
<a href="https://bing.com">Bing</a>

Your Task: Complete the link by filling in the URL and closing tag. Point the link to 'https://google.com'.
initialCode:
Welcome to my Website

About Me

I am a student learning HTML today.

Search on Google
successMsg: Excellent! You created a hyperlink.

title: Images
instruction:
The <img> tag inserts an image. It uses the src attribute for the image file and the alt attribute for a short description.

Example:
<img src="photo.png" alt="A sunset">

Your Task: Add an image tag with the source sunset.png and alt text Sunset.
successMsg: Excellent! Images make a page more interesting.

title: Bullet Lists
successMsg: Great! You created a list structure.

title: The HTML Tag
instruction:
The document structure is almost complete. The DOCTYPE and the existing <head> and <body> content are already there, so your only job is to add the missing outer <html> opening and closing tags.

Your Task: Wrap the current <head> and <body> sections in a single <html> element.
initialCode:
Welcome to my Website

About Me

I am a student learning HTML today.

Search on Google
successMsg: Almost done! You've given your page its correct document wrapper.

title: The Head Tag
instruction:
The page already has an <html> wrapper and the visible content, so only the metadata section is missing. Add the missing <head> opening and closing tags around the page title so it appears before the content.

Your Task: Place the existing <title> inside a <head> section.
initialCode:
Welcome to my Website

About Me

I am a student learning HTML today.

Search on Google
validate: return code.toLowerCase().includes('<head>') && code.toLowerCase().includes('</head>') && code.toLowerCase().includes('<title>') && code.toLowerCase().indexOf('</head>') < code.toLowerCase().indexOf('<body>');
successMsg: Good job! Your page now has the metadata it needs.

title: The Body Tag
instruction:
The page already has the proper <html> and <head> sections, so only the visible content wrapper is missing. Add the missing <body> opening and closing tags around the heading, paragraph, and link.

Your Task: Wrap your current visible content inside a single <body> section.
initialCode:
Welcome to my Website

About Me

I am a student learning HTML today.

Search on Google
successMsg: Great! Your content is now safely inside the body.

title: Document Type
instruction:
The page structure is already complete, so only the very first line is missing. Add the <!DOCTYPE html> declaration at the top of the document so the browser knows it's reading an HTML5 page.

Your Task: Add the DOCTYPE declaration at the very top of your document.
initialCode:
Welcome to my Website

About Me

I am a student learning HTML today.

Search on Google
validate: return code.toLowerCase().includes('<!doctype html>') && code.toLowerCase().indexOf('<!doctype html>') < code.toLowerCase().indexOf('<html>');
successMsg: Congratulations! You built a structurally perfect HTML document!

title: Styling with CSS
instruction:
You can change how things look using the style attribute inside your HTML tags.

Example:
<h1 style="color: blue;">Hello</h1>

Your Task: Add one CSS style to this heading so the text color becomes 'blue'.
successMsg: Nice! You just used Inline CSS to style your element.

title: Multiple CSS Properties
instruction:
You can add more than one CSS property to the same style attribute by separating them with semicolons.

Example:
<h1 style="color: red; text-align: center;">Hello</h1>

Your Task: Keep the existing background color and add two new properties to the heading: color: blue and text-align: center.
validate: return code.toLowerCase().includes('style=') && code.toLowerCase().includes('color: blue') && code.toLowerCase().includes('text-align: center') && code.toLowerCase().includes('background-color: yellow');
successMsg: Excellent! You combined multiple CSS properties.

title: Text Alignment
instruction:
The text-align property controls where text appears horizontally on the page.

Example:
<p style="text-align: center;">Hello</p>

Your Task: Add a text-align: center style to the paragraph so it appears centered.
successMsg: Great! Your text is now centered.

title: Font Size and Family
instruction:
Use font-size to change how big text looks and font-family to change the type of font.

Example:
<h2 style="font-size: 24px; font-family: serif;">Title</h2>

Your Task: Make the heading larger with font-size: 24px and change the font to serif.
successMsg: Awesome! You changed both the size and style of the font.

title: Background Color
instruction:
The background-color property changes the background color of an element. Apply it to the whole page so the website has a colored background instead of the default white look.

Example:
<body style="background-color: lightblue;">...</body>

Your Task: Add a background-color style to the <body> tag.
initialCode: Hello World!
successMsg: Great! You added background-color to your styling toolkit.

title: Internal Style Sheets
instruction:
Instead of styling every single tag inline, you can use Internal CSS. By adding a <style> tag inside your <head>, you can style all elements of a certain type at once!

Example:
<style>
  p { color: green; }
</style>

Your Task: Add a <style> tag inside the <head> and set the color of all <h1> tags to 'red'.
successMsg: Magaling! You just styled multiple elements at once using Internal CSS.

title: CSS Classes
instruction:
What if you only want to style some elements? Use a CSS Class! In your CSS, start a class name with a period (like .highlight). Then, add class="highlight" to your HTML tags.

Example:
.special { color: orange; }
<p class="special">Text</p>

Your Task: Create a class named .blue-text inside the <style> tag that changes the color to blue. Then, add class="blue-text" to the second paragraph.
successMsg: Nice one! Classes are reusable styles you can apply anywhere.

title: CSS IDs
instruction:
While classes can be used on many elements, an ID is for a single, unique element on the page. In CSS, IDs start with a hashtag (like #main). In HTML, you use id="main".

Example:
#header { text-align: center; }
<h1 id="header">Title</h1>

Your Task: Create an ID named #hero in the style tag that makes the text-align 'center'. Then, add id="here" to the <h1> tag.
successMsg: Astig! IDs are perfect for unique sections of your website.

title: HTML Tables
instruction:
To display data in a grid, we use tables! A table is wrapped in <table>. Inside, we create rows with <tr> (table row), and inside rows, we add cells with <td> (table data).

Example:
<table>
  <tr>
    <td>Cell 1</td>
  </tr>
</table>

Your Task: Create a table with exactly one row (<tr>) and two data cells (<td>). Put 'Apple' in the first cell and 'Banana' in the second.
validate: return code.toLowerCase().includes('<table>') && code.toLowerCase().includes('<tr>') && code.toLowerCase().includes('<td>') && code.toLowerCase().includes('apple') && code.toLowerCase().includes('banana');
successMsg: Superb! You've built your first data table.

title: Table Headers
instruction:
Tables usually have headers to describe the columns. Instead of using <td> for the top row, use <th> (table header)! This makes the text bold and centered by default.

Your Task: Change the <td> tags in the very first row to <th> tags so 'Fruit' and 'Color' become headers.
successMsg: Galing! Headers make tables much easier to read.

title: Forms & Text Inputs
instruction:
To collect user input, we use a <form> tag. Inside it, the <input> tag is a versatile element. Setting its type attribute to "text" creates a standard text box.

Example:
<form>
  Name: <input type="text">
</form>

Your Task: Wrap the username input in a <form> tag, and add an <input type="text"> next to it.
successMsg: Awesome! Now users can type their name.

title: Password & Hidden Inputs
instruction:
Forms need to keep secrets! <input type="password"> hides the characters you type (like *****). <input type="hidden"> stores data invisibly—users can't see it, but the form sends it behind the scenes.

Your Task: Add a password input next to 'Password:', and add a hidden input anywhere inside the form.
successMsg: Very secure! You're mastering form data.

title: Radio Buttons & Checkboxes
instruction:
Need choices? <input type="radio"> lets users pick only ONE option (like a multiple-choice test). <input type="checkbox"> lets users select MULTIPLE options (like choosing toppings).

Your Task: Add a radio input for 'Yes' and a checkbox input for 'I agree'.
initialCode:
Do you like coding? Yes:
I agree to terms:
successMsg: Sana all pinili, 'di ba? *umiyak* Great job!

title: File Uploads & Image Inputs
instruction:
You can upload files using <input type="file">. You can also use an image as a clickable submit button using <input type="image" src="url">!

Your Task: Add a file input to upload a document. Also, add an image input (use src="button.png" for the source).
successMsg: Amazing! You can now handle file uploads.

title: Submit, Reset, & Buttons
instruction:
Finally, we need buttons to interact with the form!
1. type="submit" sends the form data.
2. type="reset" clears all inputs back to default.
3. type="button" is a plain button you can program later with JavaScript.

Your Task: Add all three of these input types inside the form.
successMsg: Fantastic! You've learned all the core HTML form inputs!
